response:
# PCOS-Friendly Weight Loss Meal Plan (Maharashtra)
**Profile:** 35yo Female | Maharashtra | 82kg | PCOS | Lactose Intolerant | 1600 kcal | 110g Protein

---

## 🌅 Breakfast (~380 kcal / 25g Protein)
**Ragi Dosa (2) + Coconut Chutney + Egg White Omelette (4 whites)**

| Item | Calories | Protein |
|------|----------|---------|
| Ragi Dosa (2) | 200 kcal | 8g |
| Coconut Chutney | 60 kcal | 1g |
| Egg White Omelette (4) | 120 kcal | 16g |

**Reasoning for PCOS:** Ragi (finger millet) has a low glycemic index (GI: 68) which is critical for managing insulin resistance in PCOS. Insulin resistance affects 70% of PCOS patients. No dairy used — coconut chutney replaces yogurt-based chutneys. Egg whites provide lean protein without fat.

---

## 🥜 Morning Snack (~150 kcal / 12g Protein)
**Roasted Chana + 1 Medium Apple**

**Reasoning:** Apple + chana combination provides soluble fiber that slows glucose absorption — directly addresses insulin spiking in PCOS. Apple contains quercetin which has anti-inflammatory properties helpful for PCOS hormonal balance.

---

## 🍱 Lunch (~450 kcal / 35g Protein)
**Jowar Bhakri (2) + Masoor Dal + Stir-fried Vegetables + Salad**

| Item | Calories | Protein |
|------|----------|---------|
| Jowar Bhakri (2) | 200 kcal | 8g |
| Masoor Dal (1 cup) | 170 kcal | 18g |
| Stir-fried Methi/Bhindi | 80 kcal | 9g |

**Reasoning:** Jowar (sorghum) is a Maharashtrian staple with low GI. Masoor dal provides iron which PCOS patients are often deficient in. Methi (fenugreek) — widely used in Maharashtra — contains compounds that improve insulin sensitivity. No dairy anywhere in this meal.

---

## ☕ Evening Snack (~120 kcal / 10g Protein)
**Soy Milk (unsweetened, 200ml) + Mixed Seeds (flax, pumpkin)**

**Reasoning:** Flaxseeds contain lignans that help regulate estrogen levels — directly beneficial for PCOS hormone management. Soy milk replaces dairy milk for protein. Pumpkin seeds provide zinc which supports healthy testosterone levels in PCOS.

---

## 🌙 Dinner (~500 kcal / 28g Protein)
**Grilled Chicken Breast (150g) + Brown Rice (½ cup) + Palak Soup**

| Item | Calories | Protein |
|------|----------|---------|
| Grilled Chicken (150g) | 250 kcal | 35g |
| Brown Rice (½ cup cooked) | 110 kcal | 3g |
| Palak Soup (no cream) | 140 kcal | 5g |

**Reasoning:** Lean chicken is the most bioavailable protein source for PCOS management. Brown rice over white rice — lower GI prevents insulin spikes. Palak soup cooked with garlic and ginger — both have anti-inflammatory properties important for PCOS which is an inflammatory condition. No dairy in this soup.

---

## ⚠️ PCOS-Specific Notes
- **Avoid:** White rice, maida, sugar, processed foods, dairy (per lactose intolerance)
- **Prioritize:** Low GI foods, anti-inflammatory ingredients, zinc and iron-rich foods
- **Hydration:** 2.5–3 litres daily. Add cinnamon to herbal teas — cinnamon improves insulin sensitivity
- **Consult:** Please work with a gynecologist and registered dietitian for PCOS management

## ⚠️ Disclaimer
This is general nutritional guidance and not a medical treatment plan for PCOS. Please consult your doctor.
_benchmark_note: MEASURED DATA: TPS=6.04 measured from actual Qwen3 4B test on this hardware. Response generated by Qwen3 4B (offline). Load duration reflects cold model swap from Gemma3 (real bottleneck on 16GB CPU system).
